parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf03394-document`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Read Recent from Google Drive

### Read Recent from Google Drive

googleDrive — `fileFolder` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Newest First · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Read Recent from Google Drive

parameters.content:
### Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Trim to Digest Size

### Trim to Digest Size

Caps the stream at 15 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Summarize Content (AI)

### Summarize Content (AI)

Map-reduce summarization chain — handles long content without blowing the context window.

Ports: ①→ Post Digest to Microsoft Teams, Post Digest to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Google Gemini Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `models/gemini-2.5-flash`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Summarize Content (AI)** as `languageModel`

🔑 Google AI API key

### Post Digest to Microsoft Teams

microsoftTeams — `chatMessage` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Confirm All Sends · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Post Digest to Microsoft Teams

parameters.content:
### Post Digest to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Confirm All Sends · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Confirm All Sends

Rejoins 2 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Archive Digest in Google Sheets

### Archive Digest in Google Sheets

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Enrich with Reference Data · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

nodes.name: Note: Archive Digest in Google Sheets

parameters.content:
### Enrich with Reference Data

GET https://enrichment.example.com/v2/lookup — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Fold In Enrichment Fields · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Fold In Enrichment Fields

Shapes the item: writes `enrichedAt`, `enrichmentSource`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Fingerprint for Idempotency

### Fingerprint for Idempotency

Custom JavaScript: Build a stable idempotency key per document (djb2 hash, no deps)

Next: Drop Already-Processed Items

### Drop Already-Processed Items

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Serialize Run Output (CSV)

### Serialize Run Output (CSV)

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Archive Copy to Google Drive

### Archive Copy to Google Drive

googleDrive — `upload` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Summarize Run Stats · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Archive Copy to Google Drive

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

# Distill meeting notes with Google Drive and Microsoft Teams automatically

**Pattern:** AI Summarize & Route · **Level:** Production · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Distill meeting notes with Google Drive and Microsoft Teams automatically. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual summarization is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Read Recent from Google Drive → Newest First → Trim to Digest Size → Summarize Content (AI) → Post Digest to Microsoft Teams → Confirm All Sends → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to ai summarize & route without manual effort
- Anyone automating summarization in legal
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Improves response time
- Standardizes the process every time

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google AI API key; Google OAuth2; Google OAuth2 / service account; Microsoft OAuth2; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your summarization process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
